Home cooking fuels Blue Jays as team ends ugly slide with win over Phillies   On Loonie Dogs Night, the Blue Jays were in no position to stomach yet another loss.

SIMMONS: Leafs' Kyle Dubas playing dangerous guessing game on goalie Matt Murray   Kyle Dubas is banking his reputation, probably his job and possibly the future of Auston Matthews with the Maple Leafs on a goaltender who has lost his way.

GM Kyle Dubas has limited options for Maple Leafs as free agency opens   Unless Kyle Dubas makes a move that few anticipate, don’t expect the Maple Leafs general manager to make a big splash when free agency opens on Wednesday.

SIMMONS: Phillies' Canadian manager feels at home on the road at Rogers Centre   Pretty much everything about Rob Thomson said he couldn’t make it in professional baseball, least of all his Canadian passport.
Does new Maple Leafs goalie Matt Murray still have that silver touch in net?   Matt Murray isn’t the first Stanley Cup goaltender big on bringing that silver touch back to the Maple Leafs.

Elks vs Alouettes Week 6 Picks and Predictions: Edmonton Hangs Tough   Week 6 in the CFL kicks off on Thursday with a battle between struggling squads as the Montreal Alouettes host the Edmonton Elks. Montreal recently dismissed its head coach after a 1-3 start while Edmonton is 1-4.

Phillies vs Blue Jays Picks and Predictions: Toronto and Philly Pile Up Runs In Exciting Matchup   The Toronto Blue Jays come home after a rough 1-6 straight up road trip where their only win was a 2-1 victory. Now the sliding club opens up a brief two-game home set with the Philadelphia Phillies who will be going with a bullpen day. Toronto will counter with Jose Berrios.

THE OPEN: Betting picks for this week at the Old Course   ST ANDREWS, Scotland — Another major championship means another round of betting picks. Like any petty gambler, I’ll start by reminding you that my five U.S. Open picks last month included a Matthew Fitzpatrick win and an Adam Hadwin top-10. Of course, we don’t talk about the ones I got wrong. This week it’s the final major of the season: The Open Championship at the Old Course. These are my best bets, based, at least in part, on value. For instance, Rory McIlroy is playing great but at 10/1 it’s not a bet I’ll recommend.

Open Championship Sleeper Picks & Predictions: Could Louis Strike Again on Familiar Grounds?   The fourth and final major of the 2022 PGA Tour season will descend upon “the home of golf,” better known as The Old Course at St. Andrews, for the 150th Open Championship odds from July 14-17 in Scotland.

NFL Week 1 Odds: Baker Mayfield Trade Shortens Panthers-Browns Spread   We’re less than two months away from the start of the 2022 NFL season, and we now have a full slate of games to look forward to.
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.
HAVE YOUR SAY: Answer our daily Toronto Sun poll question
EDITORIAL: Trudeau’s sanctions are just hot air   Canada’s brave federal government stood proudly against Russian aggression in Ukraine by imposing strict sanctions.

CHARLEBOIS AND TAYLOR: Dealing with 'Greedflation'   Accusations of gouging in the food industry have reached an all-time high. According to a recent survey, 68% of Canadians believe food corporations are taking advantage of the inflationary cycle to increase prices, and it’s not just in retail. While both Quebec and British Columbia now have class-action lawsuits against the beef industry, many trade groups and politicians are now asking the federal government to investigate.

OPINION: Ontario tax reforms could boost business growth   Since Doug Ford’s Progressive Conservative Party won a decisive majority in the Ontario election, much of the discussion has centered around infrastructure investments, but the province’s lack of tax competitiveness should be an immediate priority for the re-elected premier.

OPINION: Pan-Canadian strategy needed in rare disease battle   More than three million Canadians with rare diseases and their families have been fighting to solve a puzzle of testing, diagnosis, care, and treatment their whole lives. Mostly on their own.
Bruce Springsteen and E Street Band skip Canada on 2023 tour   'After six years, I’m looking forward to seeing our great and loyal fans next year'
Jessica Chastain shares support for body-shamed Florence Pugh   Jessica Chastain has insisted women don’t “belong” to men.
Mickey Rourke brands Tom Cruise 'irrelevant'   Mickey Rourke has branded Tom Cruise “irrelevant.”

Johnny Depp brands Amber Heard's attempt at mistrial a 'fail'   Johnny Depp’s lawyers have a message for his ex-wife Amber Heard : it doesn’t matter if the wrong juror showed up for their contentious defamation trial, you still have to pay up.
Kate Mara confirms pregnancy via Instagram post   Kate Mara is pregnant.
Hit the road to see some spectacular Ontario spots   Should you stay or should you go?
COVID-19 stress to blame for rise in adult acne   There are plenty of things adults don’t miss about being a teenager: homework, curfews, and getting just the right level of acid wash in your jeans.
ASK AMY: Landlord wants appreciation over rent hike   Dear Amy: I have tenants I have bent over backwards for. I cut their late fees in half, and allow them to pay the rent by the 15th of each month.
Canada's laundry and personal hygiene habits leave a lot to be desired   Towels, toilets and teeth – Canadians are hiding dirty little secrets about all three
Drugmaker seeks OK for first birth control pill without prescription in U.S.   The contraceptive is currently a prescription drug sold under the brand Opill.

SUNshine Girl Carmen   SUNshine Girl Carmen is a brown-eyed adventurer who loves travel and experiencing new cultures. She also enjoys shopping, eating, and music. Carmen worked at Disney, so naturally she a fan of all things Mickey. Some day, she hopes to work in California. (Ernest Doroszuk/Toronto Sun)
SUNshine Girl Jessica   SUNshine Girl Jessica is a blue-eyed Aquarius who loves cats, modelling, hockey, lacrosse, hunting — and fast cars. (Stan Behal/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Stephanie   SUNshine Girl Stephanie loves to spend time with family, preferably at the beach. The 5-foot-7, blue-eyed Aries, who first posed for us in July 2019, also loves painting, modelling and working out. (Stan Behal/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Chelsea   Oh snap, SUNshine Girl Chelsea is back! The sunny Sagittarius likes taking photos, listening to pop music, riding motorcycles, travelling, singing and painting. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Cassandra   SUNshine Girl Cassandra is back and hopefully living the pampered life. The last time we checked in, she was dreaming of being a rich housewife and driving Bugattis. (Ernest Doroszuk/Toronto Sun)
Quebec company takes Tesla Model S Plaid to 347 km/h   Software hacks permitted the car to blow past its 175-mph (281-km/h) limiter and crack the vaunted 200-mph barrier
Watch: Pilot lands disabled plane on North Carolina highway   Sheriff says it's "amazing" the man managed to dodge vehicles and power lines
Ka-Chow: Ford trademarks 'Lightning' for Ranger, Maverick in Europe   Could the filings suggest EV variants of these models? What else could a Ranger Lightning and Maverick Lightning be?
Walmart to electrify its delivery fleet with Canoo EVs   The deal gives Walmart an option to purchase up to 10,000 units
Family Vehicle Roundup: 2023 Honda HR-V, best rides for camping, and more   Because where are else are you going to find a list of the most famous RVs from the big screen?

Drinking alone when you're young linked to alcohol problems later in life   Researchers say young people often drink alone as a way to cope with negative emotions, a pattern of use that has been linked to alcohol use disorder.

Arthritis is so much more than just a few aches and pains   Since being diagnosed with idiopathic arthritis, Rachel Gehue has had fusions on both of her wrists, and her foot, as well as splints made for the fingers on both hands.

'Nature's life support system': Fake or real, houseplants are great for mental health   Since COVID began, Canada's indoor plant industry has been booming, with sales of potted plants jumping from more than $903 million in 2020 to more than $995 million in 2021.
Does mindfulness meditation really reduce pain?   Participants taught to meditate before experiencing painful stimuli reported a 33% drop in pain unpleasantness and 32% reduction in intensity.
What are the signs of Magnesium deficiency?   More than one-third of Canadian adults aren't getting enough magnesium.
Canadian company launches pocket-sized electronic dispenser for liquid concentrates   The company-described “Keurig of cannabis” measures cannabis concentrates in 1 mg intervals.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
Ozzy Osbourne drops $1k at cannabis dispensary and then decides not to smoke   The Black Sabbath frontman has been sober for almost a decade.
Ontario police seize cannabis worth $61 million   Estimated 45,000-plus illegal pot plants and eight metric tonnes of processed weed confiscated.
Brittney Griner in the spotlight during WNBA All-Star game   “We are not going to stop until everyone understands how serious this really is.”
